name: Os Mazos and Neira de Rei from Baralla
name_original: Os Mazos y Neira de Rei desde Baralla — circular del río Nevia
description: This difficult 18.1-mile mountain biking loop near Baralla offers varied terrain, scenic views along the Nevia River, and a significant elev
region_intro: Mountain biking around Baralla offers varied and challenging natural landscapes in the green and hilly region of Galicia. The area features mountainous terrain with significant elevations and lush forests, providing numerous hills and woodland paths for off-road cycling. River valleys, such as along the Neira River, also contribute to scenic routes. Cyclists can expect a mix of asphalt, dirt tracks, and steep paths on irregular ground, with some sections featuring stone slabs. Best mountain bike trails in Baralla The most popular mountain bike trail is Leaving from Corgo — circular route along the Chamoso River — The Biosphere Reserve Terras de Miño, a 18.1 miles (29.1 km) trail that takes 2 hours 24 minutes to complete. This moderate route explores the Biosphere Reserve Terras de Miño. Another top favourite among local mountain bikers is Os Mazos and Neira de Rei from Baralla, a difficult 18.1 miles (29.2 km) path. This route features substantial elevation gain and varied terrain. Local mountain bikers also love the Alto das Penas desde Baleira — circular por la Reserva de Biosfera Río Eo, a 18.3 miles (29.5 km) trail leading through the Río Eo Biosphere Reserve, often completed in about 3 hours 1 minute. Mountain biking around Baralla is defined by mountainous terrain, lush forests, and river valleys. The network offers options for moderate and difficult ability levels. The guide includes highly-rated routes that showcase the best of the region's landscape. More than 10 mountain bikers have used komoot to explore Baralla's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on mountain bike trails around Baralla?
A: Mountain biking around Baralla offers a varied and challenging natural landscape. You'll encounter mountainous terrain with significant elevations and lush, indigenous forests. Trails often feature a mix of asphalt, dirt tracks, and steep paths on irregular ground, with some sections including stone slabs. The region's natural ruggedness, particularly along routes like the San Xil variant of the Camino de Santiago, provides demanding ascents and technical descents.

Q: How many mountain bike trails are available in the Baralla area?
A: There are &lt;strong&gt;14 mountain bike trails&lt;/strong&gt; documented around Baralla on komoot. These routes cater to various skill levels, with 9 classified as moderate and 5 as difficult.

Q: Are there any circular mountain bike routes in Baralla?
A: Yes, several circular mountain bike routes are available. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1210315093/leaving-from-corgo-circular-route-along-the-chamoso-river-the-biosphere-reserve-terras-de-mino" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Leaving from Corgo — circular route along the Chamoso River — The Biosphere Reserve Terras de Miño&lt;/a&gt; is a moderate 29 km option. Another popular choice is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1210479788/alto-das-penas-desde-baleira-circular-por-la-reserva-de-biosfera-rio-eo" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Alto das Penas desde Baleira — circular por la Reserva de Biosfera Río Eo&lt;/a&gt;, also a moderate route covering 29.5 km. For a more challenging circular ride, consider the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1210569136/mosteiro-de-santa-maria-de-penamaior-from-becerrea-circular" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Mosteiro de Santa María de Penamaior from Becerreá — circular&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: What are some challenging mountain bike routes for experienced riders?
A: Experienced riders looking for a challenge can tackle routes with substantial elevation gains. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1210606295/os-mazos-and-neira-de-rei-from-baralla" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Os Mazos and Neira de Rei from Baralla&lt;/a&gt; route is a difficult 29.2 km path with over 1000 meters of elevation gain. Another demanding option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1210459401/quinta-rio-donsal-trail" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Quintá-Río Donsal Trail&lt;/a&gt;, a difficult 33.2 km route featuring over 1200 meters of ascent.

Q: Are there any natural features or landmarks to see along the mountain bike trails?
A: Yes, the Baralla area is rich in natural beauty and historical landmarks. Along your rides, you might encounter the ancient &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/424987" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Ancient Chestnut Tree in Ramil&lt;/a&gt;. The region is also part of the Camino de Santiago, with points of interest like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4853733" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Pilgrim Monument in Triacastela&lt;/a&gt; and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4750127" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Camino de Santiago — fork at Triacastela&lt;/a&gt;. Additionally, you can find historical religious buildings such as the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4439474" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Church of Saint Mary of Vilabade&lt;/a&gt; and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4530231" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Ermita del Carmen de Vilabade&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: What is the best time of year for mountain biking in Baralla?
A: Baralla, located in the green region of Galicia, is generally pleasant for mountain biking during spring, summer, and early autumn. The lush forests and river valleys are particularly scenic during these months. However, given the 'more mountainous' nature of some routes and the potential for 'steep paths on irregular ground' and 'stone slabs,' conditions can vary. Always check local weather forecasts, especially for rain, which can make technical sections more challenging.

Q: What are the typical distances and durations for mountain bike trails in Baralla?
A: Most mountain bike trails in Baralla are designed for longer rides. The routes generally range from approximately 29 km to 33 km in distance. Depending on the difficulty and your pace, these trails can take anywhere from 2.5 hours to over 3.5 hours to complete. For instance, the moderate &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1210315093/leaving-from-corgo-circular-route-along-the-chamoso-river-the-biosphere-reserve-terras-de-mino" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Leaving from Corgo&lt;/a&gt; route is about 29 km and takes around 2 hours 24 minutes, while the difficult &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1210459401/quinta-rio-donsal-trail" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Quintá-Río Donsal Trail&lt;/a&gt;, at 33.2 km, can take over 3.5 hours.

Q: Are there any family-friendly mountain bike trails in Baralla?
A: While Baralla's terrain is generally described as varied and challenging, with many routes featuring significant elevation and technical sections, some moderate routes might be suitable for families with older, experienced children. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1210315093/leaving-from-corgo-circular-route-along-the-chamoso-river-the-biosphere-reserve-terras-de-mino" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Leaving from Corgo — circular route along the Chamoso River — The Biosphere Reserve Terras de Miño&lt;/a&gt; is a moderate 29 km route that explores a biosphere reserve, offering scenic views. Always assess the specific route details, including elevation and surface, to ensure it matches your family's skill and endurance levels.

Q: What do other mountain bikers say about the trails in Baralla?
A: Many mountain bikers have used komoot to explore Baralla's varied terrain. Users often highlight the region's natural beauty, the challenging climbs, and the rewarding descents. The mix of forest paths, dirt tracks, and occasional technical sections provides an authentic and demanding mountain biking adventure for those who appreciate natural trails.

Q: Are there options for accommodation or places to eat near the mountain bike trails in Baralla?
A: Baralla is a municipality with local amenities. You can find various accommodation options, cafes, and restaurants within the town and surrounding villages. These establishments cater to visitors and can provide a convenient base or a welcome stop for refreshments before or after your mountain biking adventures.

Q: What are the elevation gains like on Baralla's mountain bike trails?
A: Mountain bike trails in Baralla frequently involve significant elevation gains, reflecting the region's mountainous terrain. Moderate routes typically feature ascents ranging from 350 to 800 meters. For more difficult trails, you can expect substantial climbs, often exceeding 900 meters, with some routes like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1210459401/quinta-rio-donsal-trail" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Quintá-Río Donsal Trail&lt;/a&gt; reaching over 1200 meters of elevation gain.
tips: Magical arched stone bridge over the Nevia river near El Mazos. Follow the verdant landscape of the river as it flows through the valley back to Baralla.
Baralla is a small town in the Comarca de Los Ancares on the banks of the Neira River. A good place to park and start your adventures in the hills above the city.

faqs.question: What level of experience is required for the Os Mazos y Neira de Rei mountain bike route?
faqs.answer: This trail is rated as difficult and is not suitable for beginners. Riders should be in very good physical condition and have significant mountain biking experience to tackle its varied and technical demands.

faqs.question: What is the terrain like on this mountain bike route?
faqs.answer: The route features very varied terrain, characteristic of 'pure mountain bike' trails. You can expect narrow tracks with dense vegetation, occasional small but stony fords, and sections with an argillaceous sandy composition. It's a technical ride that requires good bike handling skills.

faqs.question: What notable sights or landmarks can I expect to see along the route?
faqs.answer: The trail offers scenic views along the Nevia River and passes through impressive landscapes. Keep an eye out for traditional Galician 'orreos' (granaries) and chapels in the broader region. Specific highlights include the Stone Bridge Over the Neira River at Os Mazos and the Baralla Mural of the Boy with Lollipop.

faqs.question: Where can I park to access the Os Mazos y Neira de Rei trail?
faqs.answer: The trail starts and ends in Baralla, making it a convenient base for parking. Specific parking areas are usually available within the town, though it's advisable to check local signage upon arrival for the most suitable spots for trail access.

faqs.question: Is public transport available to reach the trailhead in Baralla?
faqs.answer: Baralla is a municipality in Lugo, Galicia, and typically has bus connections to larger towns. While direct public transport to the exact trailhead might be limited, you can likely reach Baralla by bus and then cycle to the starting point of the circular route.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to mountain bike this route?
faqs.answer: Given the varied terrain and potential for dense vegetation and stony fords, spring and early autumn are generally the best times. These seasons offer milder temperatures and typically less extreme weather conditions, making the challenging ride more enjoyable. Avoid heavy rain periods, as the argillaceous sandy areas could become very muddy.

faqs.question: Is the Os Mazos y Neira de Rei circular route dog-friendly?
faqs.answer: While many trails in the region are dog-friendly, the difficulty and technical nature of this particular mountain bike route mean it might not be ideal for all dogs, especially if they are not accustomed to long, strenuous rides or varied terrain. If you do bring a dog, ensure it is well-behaved, physically capable, and kept on a leash, especially near livestock or in populated areas.

faqs.question: Do I need a permit or pay an entrance fee to ride this trail?
faqs.answer: No, there are typically no permits or entrance fees required to mountain bike the Os Mazos y Neira de Rei circular route. It is a public access trail.

faqs.question: Does the trail intersect other well-known paths?
faqs.answer: Yes, the route also passes through sections of the Camino de la Cruz de Reboredo, Camiño á Presa Fabeiro, and Ponte da Senra.

faqs.question: Are there any facilities like cafes or restaurants near the trail?
faqs.answer: Since the route starts and ends in Baralla, you'll find cafes, restaurants, and other amenities within the town itself. It's a good idea to stock up on supplies before you begin your ride, as options along the trail itself will be very limited or non-existent.

faqs.question: What should I bring for a mountain bike ride on this trail?
faqs.answer: Given its difficult rating and length, you should bring plenty of water, energy snacks, a repair kit (including spare tubes, pump, and multi-tool), appropriate safety gear (helmet, gloves), and navigation tools. Due to the varied terrain, sturdy mountain biking shoes and protective clothing are also highly recommended.
